papers.title: Attention Is All You Need
papers.abstract: The dominant sequence transduction models are based on complex recurrent or convolutional neural networks. We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms.

papers.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers
papers.abstract: We introduce BERT, a method for pre-training language representations which obtains state-of-the-art results on a wide array of natural language processing tasks.

papers.title: GPT-3: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
papers.abstract: We train GPT-3, an autoregressive language model with 175 billion parameters, and test its performance in the few-shot setting.

papers.title: Language Models are Unsupervised Multitask Learners
papers.abstract: We demonstrate that large language models, trained with sufficient data and compute, can perform well on many tasks without task-specific training.

papers.title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
papers.abstract: We study empirical scaling laws for language model performance on the cross-entropy loss.

papers.title: Neural Machine Translation by Jointly Learning to Align and Translate
papers.abstract: We propose a new architecture for neural machine translation that learns to align and translate jointly.
